definition: Chinese AI competitiveness (exemplified by DeepSeek R1 demonstrating frontier capabilities at a fraction of training cost) is a material but uncertain catalyst for capital flows. The 'captive flow premium' (3-5% of dollar overvaluation) is supported by TIC data on $200-400B cumulative portfolio flow redirection from China to US tech. Three scenarios: 25% China success, 50% stalemate, 25% China failure. The mechanism is real but magnitude and timing are highly uncertain.
evidence: DeepSeek R1: frontier-competitive at fraction of training cost
CNY strengthened ~1%, NVDA fell ~17% intraday on DeepSeek announcement
TIC data: $200-400B cumulative portfolio flow redirection from China to US tech
Dollar-tech correlation may reflect common factors, not causal chain
open_questions: Is the dollar-tech correlation causal or driven by common factors?
Would sustained Chinese AI competitiveness trigger a discontinuous captive flow reversal or gradual reallocation?
How does the US-China tech bifurcation evolve under different geopolitical scenarios?
